channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cruise Industry Remains Popular Despite Health Risks, Experts Say
item.description: The cruise industry continues to attract millions of passengers globally, despite recurring health incidents such as COVID-19, norovirus, and recent hantavirus outbreaks. Experts explain that outbreak...

item.title: Tinubu Aide Claims Many Online Peter Obi Supporters Are Bots, Few Are Real
item.description: Daniel Bwala, the Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Policy Communication, don yan say plenty supporters of former Presidential candidate Peter Obi, wey dem dey call ‘Obidients’, no really be...

item.title: WHO confirms low public risk from hantavirus after cruise ship cases
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has reassured the public that the risk of hantavirus spreading widely is very low, following the recent outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship. The ship, carryi...

item.title: Three women linked to IS face charges in Australia over alleged crimes in Syria
item.description: Three women associated with IS-linked families have been charged with serious offences after returning to Australia from Middle Eastern camps. Two of the women, aged 53 and 31, appeared separately in ...

item.title: Bwala Criticizes Opposition’s Disunity Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Public Communications and Media, Daniel Bwala, don yarn say plenty politicians wey dey opposition na 'internally displaced' and dem dey desperate for po...

item.title: Tanzania Water Ministry to Fund Projects Using Bonds and Boost Private Sector Participation
item.description: The Tanzanian government, through the Ministry of Water, has received approval from the National Assembly for a budget of 1.12 trillion shillings for the 2026/27 financial year. Minister Jumaa Aweso c...

item.title: Labour Suffers Major Losses in UK Local Elections, Starmer Stays On
item.description: Partial results from England's local elections indicate significant setbacks for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party, with gains for Nigel Farage's Reform UK party. The elections are widely int...

item.title: Armed Groups in Mali Take Local Power Amid Weak State Institutions
item.description: Mali has been struggling with political instability since 2012, when a military coup and armed groups took control of northern regions. Efforts to form transitional governments repeatedly failed, lead...

item.title: Richard Lewer Wins Archibald Prize with Portrait of Pitjantjatjara Elder Iluwanti Ken
item.description: Melbourne-based artist Richard Lewer has claimed the 2026 Archibald Prize, Australia's most prestigious portraiture award, for his life-size portrait of Pitjantjatjara Elder, senior artist, and ngangk...

item.title: Akhilesh Yadav comments on Congress exit from DMK, raising questions on INDIA alliance unity
item.description: On Friday, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav made a pointed remark on social media, seemingly directed at the Congress party, following its unexpected political move in Tamil Nadu. Sharing photogra...

item.title: Researchers Identify Method to Support Oral Health by Modifying Bacterial Communication
item.description: Researchers at the University of Minnesota have uncovered a novel approach to preventing gum disease by influencing how bacteria communicate rather than killing them. The study focuses on quorum sensi...

item.title: Aston Villa Beat Nottingham Forest 4-0 to Reach Europa League Final After 44 Years
item.description: Aston Villa don finally reach dem European final after 44 years as dem demolish Nottingham Forest 4-0 for Europa League semi-final return leg. Dem start from one-goal first-leg loss but Ollie Watkins ...

item.title: Beloved Catholic Choir Trainer John Bosco Kazoora Dies Days After Wedding
item.description: The Catholic community in Uganda is mourning the passing of John Bosco Kazoora, affectionately known as JB Kazoora, a renowned choir trainer, composer, and singer. JB Kazoora, aged 77, passed away on ...

item.title: Nakuru Man Found Dead in Menengai Crater, Family Seeks Answers
item.description: A family in Nakuru county is mourning the tragic death of Paul Ndung’u, popularly known as Kibunja, whose body was discovered in Menengai Crater under unclear circumstances. Paul, a 29-year-old boda b...

item.title: Australian Racers Paige Raddatz and Joanne Ciconte Start Kyojo Cup Season in Japan
item.description: Australian motor racing talents Paige Raddatz and Joanne Ciconte are set to make their debuts in the 2026 Kyojo Cup, an all-female racing series held at Japan's Fuji Speedway. The opening round will t...

item.title: Kano Governor Abba Yusuf submits nomination form to APC for 2027 governorship election
item.description: Governor Abba Kabir Yusuf of Kano State don officially submit him nomination form to the National Working Committee of the All Progressives Congress (APC) for him to run as the party candidate in the ...

item.title: Reform UK Leads in Local Elections as Labour Suffers Significant Losses
item.description: Early results from the local elections across parts of England indicate a clear lead for Reform UK, who are securing roughly a third of the seats declared so far. Labour, defending the most seats in t...

item.title: Senate to Begin Impeachment Trial of VP Sara Duterte Once Articles Are Transmitted
item.description: Retired Supreme Court Associate Justice Antonio Carpio clarified that under the Philippine Constitution, the Senate is mandated to convene as an impeachment court immediately once the House of Represe...

item.title: PSA Debunks False Claims of Free Electricity and Subsidies for National ID and Vaccine Card Holders
item.description: The Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) has officially debunked viral claims suggesting that holders of the national ID or COVID-19 vaccine cards can avail of free electricity and various government...

item.title: Stellantis launches Morocco centre for dismantling and recycling vehicles in Africa
item.description: Stellantis, the parent company of Peugeot and Jeep, has inaugurated its first vehicle dismantling centre in the Middle East and Africa, located in Casablanca, Morocco. The €1.6 million (R31 million) f...
